_about: Build Nervous System (Stage 4, 'Land dark') client-side feature-flag manifest. Maps a flag name to { enabled, addedInPR, description }. Read once at init time by js/build/build-flags.js into an in-memory object — no localStorage, cookies, network, or server — so it behaves identically in the browser bundle, the Tauri desktop webview, and any future Tauri mobile build. Flag hygiene follows the promote-or-delete rule in docs/build-nervous-system.md: a flag left here for more than 3 merged PRs without being promoted (removed, code kept) or reverted (removed, code deleted) must be flagged in the 4th PR that touches this manifest. `addedInPR` records where each flag's clock started.

pluginPacks.description: Gen 40 domain-pack plugin architecture: assemble the active domain-pack source from self-contained plugin modules under js/packs/ via the pack registry instead of the inline DOMAIN_PACKS map. Behaviour-identical (same runtime pack objects); flip off to fall back to the legacy inline map.

conversationalPackBuilder.description: Gen 42 guided conversational pack builder: replace the blank-text-box pack authoring with a data-grounded question flow (js/agents/question-generator-agent.js), an on-device 'I don't know' resolver (js/agents/uncertainty-resolver-agent.js), a read-only peer-pack index (js/packs/local-pack-index.js), and an incremental pack builder (js/agents/pack-builder-agent.js). Backend (#89) + UI wiring (#91) both merged and tested; flipped on 2026-07-11 for live use in the Validate tab.

conversationalPackBuilderVoice.description: Gen 42 voice input for the conversational pack builder. The free-text answer path is fully implemented and identical to voice (voice is just transcribed text). No permissively-licensed on-device WASM/WebGPU STT model is vendored yet, so the mic is gated behind this flag with a TODO: pick a small Apache/MIT WASM speech model before promotion. When off, buildQuestionView hides the mic entirely and only the typed path shows.

localAnalysisContract.description: Local Analysis Contract (js/validation/analysis-contract.js): before/alongside running a SQL query — the user's own, or one an AI assistant wrote — checks it offline against the REAL schema of the loaded dataset and flags schema hallucination (references to columns/tables that don't exist), aggregation mismatches (COUNT without DISTINCT across a JOIN; SUM of a rate-like column), and missing guard clauses (aggregate query never excludes test/deleted/refunded rows a matching column suggests it should). Flags-only, never rewrites or blocks a query — empowerment-constraint compliant. Renders as a dismissible card in #sql-result-wrap, above the result table. When off, the SQL tab behaves exactly as before this PR.

meetingScribe.description: Gen 43 meeting scribe: pure grounding/tagging logic (js/agents/meeting-scribe-agent.js, PR gen43-meeting-scribe-agent) plus a Meeting tab presenter (js/agents/meeting-scribe-ui.js, PR gen43-meeting-scribe-ui) where a person pastes or types a transcript to see pushback moments, data requests, and an action-item tracker enforcing the owner+dueDate+outcome rule. Still no audio capture or speech-to-text — those stay separate follow-up work. When this flag is off (its shipped default), renderTabBar() leaves the 'meeting' entry out of state.tabOrder entirely, so the tab is not just hidden but never added to the bar or reachable by any click path; #panel-meeting stays empty.

agentActionFirewall.description: DataGlow Passport (Batch 1) Agent Action Firewall (js/agents/agent-action-firewall.js): the single central gate every data-MUTATING code path must pass through. It classifies an action's risk/reversibility and FAILS CLOSED — a mutation runs only after an explicit per-action human confirmation that also carries an authenticated local human identity (the audit rider); there is no trusted-mode/force/auto bypass parameter anywhere. When this flag is OFF (its shipped default), main.js's Clean-tab fix handler calls clean.applyFix directly exactly as before, so runtime behaviour is unchanged; when ON, the same click routes the fix through the firewall and records who authorized it into the provenance chain. Direct regression for the April 2026 'AI agent deleted the prod DB in 9 seconds with no confirmation' incident (test/agent-action-firewall.test.mjs).
